brief.title: Founder App Idea
brief.source_text:
Founder App Idea

I want a tool that helps solo founders turn scattered notes into a cleaner execution plan.
Maybe start as a web app, but keep the first version local-first and simple.

Goals:
- Help founders clean up rough product ideas
- Highlight missing constraints before they hire engineers
- Produce a minimal delivery pack they can review locally

Non-Goals:
- No cloud sync
- Don't build team collaboration yet

Constraints:
- Budget under $15k
- Need something usable in 4 weeks
- Just me plus one contract engineer

Notes:
- I care more about speed than polish for the first release
- Reference example: https://example.com/founder-notes

artifacts.name: brief.md
artifacts.content:
# SpecForge Brief

Title: Founder App Idea
Product Type: web app
Audience: founders

## Summary
Founder App Idea is a web app aimed at founders. Primary goal: Help founders clean up rough product ideas.

## Explicit Brief Input
- Help founders clean up rough product ideas
- Highlight missing constraints before they hire engineers
- Produce a minimal delivery pack they can review locally
- No cloud sync
- Don't build team collaboration yet
- Budget under $15k
- Need something usable in 4 weeks
- Just me plus one contract engineer
- Maybe start as a web app, but keep the first version local-first and simple

## Deterministic Interpretation
- Inferred product type: web app.
- Inferred platform hints: local-first, web.
- Detected requirement overload across 3 planning area(s).

## Priority Open Questions
- Resolve contradiction: Low budget, short timeline, and an overloaded first release create an obvious delivery conflict.
- Resolve contradiction: A small team, aggressive deadline, and overloaded scope are in tension.
- Resolve contradiction: The brief calls this a simple MVP while also asking for enterprise breadth, many integrations, or multi-platform support.

## Notes
- I care more about speed than polish for the first release
- Reference example: https://example.com/founder-notes

## References
- https://example.com/founder-notes

## Source Text
```text
Founder App Idea

I want a tool that helps solo founders turn scattered notes into a cleaner execution plan.
Maybe start as a web app, but keep the first version local-first and simple.

Goals:
- Help founders clean up rough product ideas
- Highlight missing constraints before they hire engineers
- Produce a minimal delivery pack they can review locally

Non-Goals:
- No cloud sync
- Don't build team collaboration yet

Constraints:
- Budget under $15k
- Need something usable in 4 weeks
- Just me plus one contract engineer

Notes:
- I care more about speed than polish for the first release
- Reference example: https://example.com/founder-notes
```

artifacts.content:
# SpecForge Scope

## Goals
- Help founders clean up rough product ideas
- Highlight missing constraints before they hire engineers
- Produce a minimal delivery pack they can review locally
- Need something usable in 4 weeks

## Non-Goals
- No cloud sync
- Don't build team collaboration yet

## Scope Draft
- Shape the first scoped draft around a web app.
- Prioritize the needs of founders.
- Support goal: Help founders clean up rough product ideas.
- Support goal: Highlight missing constraints before they hire engineers.
- Support goal: Produce a minimal delivery pack they can review locally.
- Keep out of scope for now: No cloud sync.
- Keep out of scope for now: Don't build team collaboration yet.
- Respect platform hints: local-first, web.

## First Step Recommendation
Resolve the top unresolved question before expanding scope: Resolve contradiction: Low budget, short timeline, and an overloaded first release create an obvious delivery conflict.

artifacts.content:
# SpecForge Analysis Report

## Counts
- ambiguities: 0
- contradictions: 3
- missing_decisions: 0
- assumptions: 1
- open_questions: 3

## Ambiguities
- No ambiguity findings

## Contradictions
- [high] Fast, cheap, and feature-rich at once: Low budget, short timeline, and an overloaded first release create an obvious delivery conflict. (source_type: inferred; recommendation: Keep the first release to one core workflow and only the mandatory integrations.)
  Evidence: Maybe start as a web app, but keep the first version local-first and simple.; - Budget under $15k; - Need something usable in 4 weeks
- [high] Small team, short deadline, broad scope: A small team, aggressive deadline, and overloaded scope are in tension. (source_type: inferred; recommendation: Reduce the first release to one channel and one key workflow before committing to the deadline.)
  Evidence: Maybe start as a web app, but keep the first version local-first and simple.; - Need something usable in 4 weeks
- [high] Minimal MVP vs enterprise scope: The brief calls this a simple MVP while also asking for enterprise breadth, many integrations, or multi-platform support. (source_type: explicit; recommendation: Keep the MVP to one channel, one user role, and the minimum mandatory capability set.)
  Evidence: Maybe start as a web app, but keep the first version local-first and simple.; - Produce a minimal delivery pack they can review locally

## Missing Decisions
- No missing decisions

## Prioritized Open Questions
- Resolve contradiction: Low budget, short timeline, and an overloaded first release create an obvious delivery conflict.
- Resolve contradiction: A small team, aggressive deadline, and overloaded scope are in tension.
- Resolve contradiction: The brief calls this a simple MVP while also asking for enterprise breadth, many integrations, or multi-platform support.

artifacts.name: mvp_cut_plan.md
artifacts.content:
# SpecForge MVP Cut Plan

## Recommended MVP Cut
- Anchor the first release on this primary goal: Help founders clean up rough product ideas
- Defer secondary goals until the first workflow is validated.
- Choose one primary platform instead of splitting across: local-first, web.
- Remove enterprise-only requirements such as SSO, audit trails, or compliance-heavy scope from MVP.
- Defer secondary integrations, reporting surfaces, and admin-heavy controls to a later milestone.
- Cut the release to one role and one core workflow before keeping the stated deadline.

## Why This Is Risky
- Low budget, short timeline, and an overloaded first release create an obvious delivery conflict.
- A small team, aggressive deadline, and overloaded scope are in tension.
- The brief calls this a simple MVP while also asking for enterprise breadth, many integrations, or multi-platform support.
- A one-person team plus an explicit delivery timeline increases schedule risk.
